summary.paper_use: Use mvfst as production-relevant source/test maturity evidence with a packaged Linux focused-test gate; do not claim local mvfst execution until the runner produces an ok artifact.
summary.interpretation: mvfst has strong migration-specific source/test structure, but the current study still lacks executed Linux Buck/getdeps results.

evidence.observation: QuicPathManager is documented as managing QUIC path probing and connection migration functionality.
evidence.implication: mvfst treats migration/path probing as a first-class state-management concern.

evidence.observation: startPathProbe checks active-migration support, handshake state, socket binding, address family, adds a path, schedules PATH_CHALLENGE, and assigns a destination CID.
evidence.implication: mvfst has an explicit client active-probe flow that is richer than passive rebinding only.

evidence.observation: migrateConnection switches the current path, optionally resets congestion/RTT, emits qlog/stat migration hooks, and sends a ping to trigger migration.
evidence.implication: The client transport has an active path switch execution path.

evidence.observation: Server-side migration handles validated/fallback paths, NAT rebinding detection, qlog update, congestion state, and current path switch.
evidence.implication: mvfst has server-side passive migration logic that must be tested separately from client active migration.
The path-manager primitive tests can be addressed as a focused target on a Buck-capable host.
Client active migration can be tested without running the entire mvfst suite if Buck is available.
Server passive migration can be tested as a focused target.

evidence.observation: Tests cover challenge lookup and challenge preparation, including nonexistent and already-validated path cases.
evidence.implication: Focused path-manager coverage includes path-validation primitives that underpin migration.

evidence.topic: Client path probe and migration tests
evidence.observation: Tests cover path probe success with and without migration, current-path switch, and probe timeout.
evidence.implication: Focused client tests exercise the active probe/migrate boundary directly.

evidence.observation: Tests include client port-change NAT rebinding and client address-change NAT rebinding cases.
evidence.implication: Focused server tests cover passive migration and NAT rebinding behavior.

reporting_boundary.safe_claim: mvfst has dedicated path manager, client active probe/migration, server passive migration/NAT rebinding logic, focused BUCK targets, and 106 observed migration/path-related test cases in the readiness map.
reporting_boundary.unsafe_claim: Local mvfst build/test PASS, browser handover success, production Meta deployment behavior, or equal controllability to the quic-go AddPath/Probe/Switch positive control.
reporting_boundary.next_non_iphone_gate: Run harness/scripts/run-mvfst-focused-migration-tests-linux.sh on a Linux host with buck2 and enough disk; accept only validation=ok with all three focused BUCK targets exiting 0.
